content.plain_english: This week your child is using maths words to describe where things are and how they move — left, right, forwards, backwards, above, below. They are also meeting the idea of a turn.
content.in_school: Children play simple movement games on the carpet — 'stand up, turn around, take three steps forwards, sit down'. They use small toys and arrows to describe routes. They practise the words 'left' and 'right' with their own hands.
content.home_activity: On the walk home, take turns giving each other directions — 'go forwards three steps, now turn left, now stop'. At home, place a soft toy on a table and ask your child to describe where it is using above, below and next to. Two minutes is plenty.
dinner_table_questions: Look at the salt and pepper. Which one is on the left?
If I face the kitchen door and turn around, what am I looking at now?
Describe how to get from your bedroom to the bathroom using forwards, left and right.

key_vocabulary.definition: The side of your body where your heart sits.
Moving in the direction you are facing.
A movement that changes the way something is facing.

example_questions: Stand up. Now turn so you face the kitchen. Which way did you turn?
Is the cup on the left or the right of the plate?
How would you get from the front door to the kitchen using only 'forwards' and 'turn'?
content.times_table_tip: Year 1 children practise counting in 2s, 5s and 10s. Try counting in 2s as you take steps on the way to school — each footstep is one more two.
